title: Campylocentrum jamaicense
taxonomy: Campylocentrum jamaicense is classified as a species of orchid, specifically within the genus Campylocentrum. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae, which comprises over 30,000 known species of orchids. The genus Campylocentrum is a small group of orchids that are native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Campylocentrum jamaicense is native to Jamaica, and its classification as a species within this genus reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history. As a member of the Orchidaceae family, Campylocentrum jamaicense is closely related to other orchids, such as Vanda and Phalaenopsis.
